<Meta title="Utilities/Flex" />

# Flex

Lift provides a collection of convenience CSS classes that
will apply CSS flex properties to an element.
These allow you to easily use flexbox to create complex layouts without having to add a stylesheet
or resort to inline styling.

These classes are of the format `atp-`, followed by the name of the CSS property,
followed by a dash, followed by the CSS value.
For instance, to add `display: inline-flex` to an element,
give it the CSS class `atp-display-inline-flex`.

These classes are available for:

- `display: flex` and `display: inline-flex`
- `flex-direction`
- `flex-wrap`
- `justify-content`
- `align-content`
- `align-items`
- `align-self`

Additionally, we support the `gap` property, with values corresponding to the "t-shirt sizes"
of our spacing system. For instance, to give an element a gap of spacing size M, give it CSS
class `atp-gap-m`.
